credentialRequirements.name: IOS_SIGNING_CERT
credentialRequirements.description: iOS distribution certificate (.p12) for App Store signing
credentialRequirements.obtainedFrom: Apple Developer Program — Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles

credentialRequirements.name: ANDROID_KEYSTORE
credentialRequirements.obtainedFrom: Generated via keytool during initial Android release setup

credentialRequirements.name: APP_STORE_CONNECT_API_KEY
credentialRequirements.description: App Store Connect API key for automated submission

credentialRequirements.name: PLAY_STORE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON
credentialRequirements.description: Google Play service account JSON for automated submission
credentialRequirements.obtainedFrom: Google Cloud Console — Service Accounts, enabled for Play Developer API

approvalGate.blockedReason: App store submission requires code signing certificates, provisioning profiles, and store API credentials. These are developer account credentials that require explicit provisioning. No credentials supplied in proof pass.
requiredUserActions: Provision iOS signing certificate and provisioning profile via Apple Developer Program
Supply Android keystore and keystore password securely
Supply App Store Connect API key
Supply Google Play service account JSON

secretPolicyChecks.description: No .env files with real credential values committed to source
secretPolicyChecks.detail: No committed .env files with real credential values detected for mobile_app

secretPolicyChecks.description: Environment variable manifest (.env.example or app.config.json) is present
All required credentials declare mustNotBeCommitted=true for secrets
secretPolicyChecks.detail: mustNotBeCommitted=true is declared for all secret-class credentials in the credential manifest.

secretPolicyChecks.description: No hardcoded secret patterns (API keys, JWT tokens, AWS keys) found in source files
secretPolicyChecks.detail: No hardcoded secret patterns detected in mobile_app source files

caveat: This manifest declares credential requirements for approved credentialed deployment. No credentials are stored, validated, or used in this proof pass. Live deployment is blocked by default and requires explicit user approval and user-supplied credentials.
